_comment: Functional-certification oracles. Each entry tells CertRunner how to PROVE a setting actually gates behavior: flip it, dispatch the route, assert the feature-disabled error code appears when OFF and is absent when ON. Data-independent — the gate fires before any row lookup. This is the only hand-authored file; everything else is derived from audit/manifest.json. Grow it via `wp buddynext cert learn` — every new bug becomes a permanent row here.
_uncovered_by_design: These FeatureRegistry surfaces have NO in-band feature-gated REST route, so the flip-and-dispatch oracle pattern cannot cert them (verified 2026-06-19). Leaving them as oracles would be flaky/false. spaces: routes are always-on (no is_enabled('spaces') gate on SpaceController). sidebar/onboarding: render-only — the feature is checked in page-render/listeners, not the REST controller. gamification/wpmediaverse/career_board: integration bridges register no REST route (listeners + nav/profile-tab only). jetonomy/webhooks: only registration-gated (route absent when OFF → rest_no_route), which does not re-fire at cert time since routes register once at boot. Cover these via browser/coverage walks, not the toggle oracle.
